titleStmt.title: The American Consular Representative at Antofagasta ( Bonnet ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The American Consular Representative at Antofagasta ( Bonnet ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 26. From Warren.For explanatory information regarding Warren’s trip to South America, see circular telegram, April 23, 3 p.m., infra. “The Minister of Marine of ArgentinaPresumably Rear Adm. Alberto Teisaire. yesterday in a letter to Vice Admiral MunroeCommander of the U.S. Fourth Fleet. accepted the basis of collaboration between the two navies including exchange of bases, mutual information and the other points proposed by Admiral Munroe in his letter to the Minister. The Minister of War last night requested Generals Brett and EdwardsLt. Gen. George H. Brett, Commanding General, Caribbean Defense Command, and Maj. Gen. I. H. Edwards, respectively. to give all possible help in expediting an Argentine request to be transmitted through our Embassy at Buenos Aires today that staff conversations be initiated and American personnel made available for training program along the same lines as in other American Republics.

Vice President Perón with other ranking members of the Government, including the President’s senior aide accompanied the members of the mission to the airport this morning for their 7:30 a.m. takeoff. General Brett and party are returning to the Canal Zone by way of Rio de Janeiro and the east coast.

The economic conversations that are to be carried on by McClintockJohn C. McClintock, Special Assistant to Assistant Secretary of State Rockefetter. next week with the Minister of CommerceGen. Julio A. Checci. will be closely related to the organization of strict control measures over German banks and commercial houses.” [Warren.]
